# Transcrição: 2_ways_to_deal_with_an_INTENSE_boss

- **Fonte:** 2_ways_to_deal_with_an_INTENSE_boss
- **Duração:** 01:12

## [00:00 - 01:12]

As a listener, if they're not aware, how can you make them aware that they need to tone it down? This is when they're using a very, they're being a little bit intense. The first thing, it's not always advised, it depends on context, depending on the authority level you have in that situation and everything. Please don't do this, otherwise, you know, some of you can get fired. What you do is, for a moment, you mirror what they're doing, just to give them a sense of what's coming through. I can see what you're saying, James, and I know that it's frustrating you. And sometimes, by just quickly giving them a glimpse of what they're giving you, quickly helps them readjust. So, I'm feeling everything you're saying, right? And then, all of a sudden, they get a bit of a sense of it, and if they've got good EQ, they bring it down. If they don't have good EQ, then it's important not to bring it up there and then. Because sometimes, in the moment of the emotions, they can be irrational. But you can talk to them after, and just go, hey, you were talking to me before, you know, yesterday, just during lunch. It just felt really full on. Was there something underlying the message? Were you angry at me? Were you upset at me? I just wanted to check, because it felt really intense. And when you give time and space, and then you approach the behavior, it's done so with less emotional volatility. So, I'll either, sometimes, if it's appropriate, I mirror them a bit so they can notice it. Failing that, I talk to them the day after. Time and space.
